TRIAL IS ADVANCED TO OCT-7-2013 AT 11:15A.M. IN DEPT 503. GIVEN THAT THIS TRIAL DATE IS 55 DAYS FROM THIS HEARING, PLAINTIFFS DO STIPULATE THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BY FILED AND SERVED ON SHORTENED NOTICE SUCH THAT NOTICE REQUIRED IS 15 CALENDAR DAYS WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR ELECTRONIC SERVICE, OPPOSITIONS ARE DUE 5 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLIES ARE DUE 2 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. BEFORE ANY MOTIONS IN LIMINE ARE FILED WITH THE COURT THE DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER ON ALL MILS IN ORDER TO AVOID FRIVOLOUS AND DUPLICATIVE HEARINGS. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS OCT-3. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30PM ON OCT-3-2013. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS SEP-20. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS OCT-4. BY SEPTEMBER 9, THE PARTIES MUST NOTIFY THE COURT IN WRITING TO CONFIRM THE NEED FOR THE C.M.C. IF THE COURT DOES NOT HEAR FROM THE PARTIES THE C.M.C. WILL GO OFF CALENDAR. IF THERE IS A C.M.C, THE PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT BY TELEPHONE. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO S. F. LOCAL RULES OF COURT RULE 5 ON OCTOBER 7, 2013 AT 1:30PM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 5.0 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY OCTOBER 3, 2013 AT 2:00P.M. 25% to AHARI LEE GILLEN (Daughter)
17.5 % to DIANNA SMITH (Daughter)
2.5% to JORDEN ADAMS, a Minor (grandson)
2.5% to TANNER ADAMS, a Minor (grandson)
2.5% to KRYSTA ADAMS, a Minor (grandson)

3. 4. Petitioner is authorized to deposit additional funds as they become available, withoiut limitation. The financial institution shall accept such deposits without need for additional orders of the Court.
Orders signed in open Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial; Advancing Current Trial Date; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Kay & Janace Lindahl

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS KAY & JANACE LINDAHL'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL; ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Prior to the hearing, counsel for plaintiffs stipulated to the filing and serving of summary motions on shortened notices as requested by the Court.
No appearance at the hearing and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a), (d). No opposition filed. Trial is advanced from March 24, 2014 to October 7, 2013 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Given that this trial date is 55 days from this hearing, the court requests and plaintiffs do stipulate that summary motions may by filed and served on shortened notice such that notice required is 15 calendar days without an additional two days for electronic service, oppositions are due 5 court days before the hearing and replies are due 2 court days before the hearing. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all M.I.L.s in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the M.I.Ls the court will take appropriate actions. Last day for hearing summary motions is October 3. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than August 30. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 6. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on October 3, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 20. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 4. No later than August 28, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than August 28, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further C.M.C. on September 11, 2013 at 1:30pm. By September 9, the parties must notify the court in writing to confirm the need for the C.M.C. If the court does not hear from the parties the C.M.C. will go off calendar. If there is a C.M.C, the parties may appear at by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on October 7, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by October 3, 2013 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
